title: 001.6 — Data as Sediment: What Accumulates When Nobody's Looking
subtitle: The logs grow. The tables accrete. No one asked them to.

title: 005.13 — Learning Your Third Language Is Different Than Your First

subtitle: Beneath your Kubernetes cluster there is metal. Beneath the metal, agreements.

subtitle: The database doesn't sleep. It waits.

title: 010.0 — Documentation as Love Letter to the Future
subtitle: The README is addressed to someone you haven't met yet.

subtitle: The ecosystem metaphor is useful because ecosystems have predators.

title: 006.3 — The Model Doesn't Know It Doesn't Know
subtitle: Confidence is not accuracy. The model has learned to sound sure.

title: 174.9 — On Engineering Ethics When Nobody Enforces Them
subtitle: Other professions have boards. Software engineering has the vibes of your immediate team.

subtitle: The history of Western philosophy is a series of merge conflicts nobody fully resolved.

subtitle: The pull request is a power structure wearing the clothes of a workflow.

title: 025.04 — Search Is a Politics
subtitle: What surfaces in a search result is not neutral.

title: 070.4 — Technical Writing Is Still Writing
subtitle: Prose is not decoration on top of technical content.

title: 006.7 — Version Control as Memory: What Git Actually Stores
subtitle: Git doesn't store diffs. It stores snapshots.

title: 152.4 — Why Anger Is a Better Bug Report Than Confusion
subtitle: Confusion says: I don't understand. Anger says: I understand and it's wrong.

title: 153.4 — Pattern Recognition Is Not Always Your Friend
subtitle: The brain is a pattern-matching engine that cannot turn itself off.

title: 160.0 — Logic Is a Tool, Not a Worldview
subtitle: Formal logic is a precision instrument for evaluating internal consistency.

title: 302.3 — Networks as Infrastructure of Trust
subtitle: Every network protocol is a formalized agreement about who trusts whom.

title: 150.1 — Debugging as Dissociation: Where You Go When You're In It
subtitle: There is a particular mental state that arrives about forty minutes into a hard debugging session.

subtitle: The singleton pattern says: there is one instance, and everything must go through it.

title: 153.6 — The Intrusive Thought and the Compiler Error
subtitle: The compiler error arrives uninvited. The intrusive thought arrives uninvited.

title: 510.0 — The Proof Doesn't Care About You
subtitle: Mathematics is the one domain where the argument either holds or it doesn't.

title: 519.5 — Probabilistic Thinking for Deterministic Minds
subtitle: Computers do exactly what you tell them. This trains programmers to expect deterministic outcomes.

title: 306.4 — The Culture of the Terminal
subtitle: The terminal is not neutral territory. It is a cultural artifact from a specific tradition.

title: 305.0 — Whose Defaults? On Bias Baked Into Systems
subtitle: Every system has defaults. Defaults are not neutral.

title: 331.0 — On Unpaid Labor in Open Source
subtitle: The open source economy runs on labor that is structurally unpaid.

title: 400.0 — Language Without a Spec

subtitle: There is a pattern in software discourse of reaching for physics metaphors.

subtitle: The two hard things in computer science are cache invalidation and naming things.

subtitle: The Terms of Service are a legally binding contract you agreed to without reading.

title: 570.0 — The Cell as Model: What Biology Taught Distributed Systems
subtitle: The cell solved distributed computing three billion years before we named the problem.

title: 590.0 — The Behavior of Organisms and the Behavior of APIs
subtitle: The API is an interface. The organism is also an interface.

title: 600.0 — Engineering Is Applied Compromise
subtitle: The optimal engineering solution is the one that correctly accounts for the constraints.

title: 418.0 — Code Comments as Translation Artifacts
subtitle: A comment is a translation between two languages: code and intent.

subtitle: The code compiles. The code does not do what you thought it did.

title: 363.7 — The Environmental Cost of Your Build Pipeline
subtitle: The CI/CD pipeline runs on electricity that comes from somewhere.

subtitle: Attention is the resource being extracted from you, at scale, by systems you helped build.

subtitle: The glossary determines what can be thought inside the system.

subtitle: Most working developers are using math they learned well enough to pass exams.

subtitle: The meeting has a specific use case. It is not the use case you're using it for.

subtitle: Ask a developer what they listen to while coding and you learn something about how they think.

subtitle: There is a specific frustration that comes from being managed by someone who doesn't understand what you do.

subtitle: Russian literature does not believe in tidy resolutions.

subtitle: The programmer's first impulse is to say that code is not writing.

subtitle: The recipe is an imperative program for producing food.

subtitle: You thought you didn't care about color theory. Then you tried to pick a status color.

subtitle: The design system is infrastructure that nobody thinks about until it breaks.

subtitle: The hammer that fits the hand well is beautiful.

subtitle: American literature is obsessed with reinvention and the blank slate.

subtitle: The architecture diagram almost always lies.

subtitle: The Roman road network is still in use.

subtitle: The half-life of technical knowledge in this field is short.

subtitle: Attention degrades over the course of the day.
